changes: Rewrote skill from verbose 434-line document to concise ~60-line targeted version
Added critical rule: work in current directory, never use ~/Downloads or $HOME paths
Added macOS-specific instructions: use md5 not md5sum, stat -f not stat --format
Provided exact working md5 duplicate detection recipe with correct output parsing
Instructed model to analyze md5 output manually instead of using broken uniq -d pipes
Added rule to list all files with single find command before acting
Fixed zsh glob expansion issue: zsh errors on unmatched globs even with 2>/dev/null
Removed glob-based mv commands (mv dir/*.ext dest/) that fail in zsh
Added explicit warning that mv -t does not exist on macOS
Provided shell script with case statement to move files by extension safely
Added individual mv per file as alternative approach
Added -maxdepth 1 to initial find to only list files in target directory root

root_causes_fixed: Model searched ~/Downloads (real home dir) instead of workspace downloads/ directory
Model used md5sum (Linux) instead of md5 (macOS)
Model used uniq -d on md5 output which never matches because each line includes filename
Model used mv -t (GNU coreutils) which doesn't exist on macOS
Model used glob-based mv (*.ext) which zsh rejects when no matches found
Model got stuck in infinite loops repeating failed commands
